title: Xtra Sweet Brazilian Rice Pilaf
description: A fluffy, sweet, festive rice pilaf with a hint of citrus from white wine vinegar, perfect as a dessert or side.
instructions: Rinse 2 cups of long grain white rice until water runs clear, then set aside.
In a large pot, heat 2 tbsp olive oil over medium heat and sauté diced red onion, garlic, sweet potato, carrot, and bell pepper until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the rinsed rice to the pot and stir to coat the grains with the oil and vegetables for 2 minutes.
Pour in 4 cups vegetable broth and 1 cup coconut milk, then stir in 2 tbsp white wine vinegar and 2 tbsp maple syrup.
Bring the mixture to a gentle boil, then cover and reduce heat to low, simmering for 18 minutes or until the rice is tender and liquid is mostly absorbed.
Remove the pot from heat, fluff the rice with a fork, and let it rest covered for 5 minutes.
Stir in chopped parsley, season with salt and pepper to taste, and if the mixture is too thick add a splash of broth.
Transfer the pilaf to a serving dish, drizzle with a little more maple syrup if desired, and garnish with extra parsley.
Serve warm as a sweet side or dessert accompaniment, and enjoy!
